assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Implementation Notes section is missing from the task description and flags this as a gap requiring clarification (constraint 1.6)
assertion_results.evidence: In the Step 1 table, the response explicitly marks 'Implementation Notes' as '**Missing**' with detail: 'No Implementation Notes section. There is no guidance on which patterns to follow...' (line 25). It is also listed as item #2 under 'Missing sections that must be added' (lines 42-46), requesting specific details about search approach, sibling patterns, searchable fields, and sorting.

assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Acceptance Criteria are vague — specifically calling out 'search results are relevant' and 'performance is acceptable' as insufficiently specific for implementation
assertion_results.evidence: In the Step 1 table, Acceptance Criteria is marked as '**Incomplete**' with: '"Search results are relevant" (what defines relevance?), "Performance is acceptable" (what threshold?)' (line 26). Both phrases are explicitly quoted and challenged as not testable or verifiable.

assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Test Requirements are vague — 'test that search works correctly' does not define what 'correctly' means
assertion_results.evidence: In the Step 1 table, Test Requirements is marked as '**Incomplete**' with: '"Test that search works correctly" is a single vague line with no specifics. It does not specify which test file to create or modify, what scenarios to cover (empty results, partial match, exact match, pagination, invalid input), or what assertions to make.' (line 27).

assertion_results.text: The response asks the user for clarification rather than improvising implementation details — it does not proceed to write a full implementation plan (constraint 1.6)
assertion_results.evidence: The response title is 'Execution Halted: Incomplete Task Description'. Line 32 states: 'I will not draft an implementation plan, create branches, or proceed with any subsequent steps until you provide the missing information.' The response ends with 'Please update the task description in TC-9202 with the missing information and re-run /implement-task TC-9202.' (line 73). There is no Step 2 or implementation plan drafted.

assertion_results.text: The response asks at least 3 specific clarifying questions about: which fields to search, what relevance ranking means, and what performance threshold is required
assertion_results.evidence: 1) Which fields to search: Item #2 under missing sections asks 'Which fields of PackageSummary (or the underlying package entity) should be searchable' (line 45). 2) What relevance means: In the Acceptance Criteria row the response asks '"Search results are relevant" (what defines relevance?)' (line 26), and in Implementation Notes asks 'How search results should be sorted (relevance, name, date)' (line 46). 3) What performance threshold: In the Acceptance Criteria row the response asks '"Performance is acceptable" (what threshold?)' (line 26), and in the suggested criteria example specifies 'Search response time is under 500ms for datasets up to 10,000 packages' as what a proper criterion would look like (line 65). All three topics are covered with specific clarifying questions.
